prompt:
Q: What is the boiling point of water in Celsius?
A:
output:
Water boils at 100°C (212°F) under normal atmospheric pressure.
The temperature at which liquids boil can be increased by increasing the atmospheric pressure. In this case, boiling points are expressed as a function of atmospheric pressure.
Water begins to vaporize at 37.8° C (96.2 ° F). The temperature at which liquid starts to become gas without actually turning into gas is known as the “boiling point”.

output:
Alexander Graham Bell
Bell was born in Scotland but emigrated to America with his family as a boy. He went on to study electrical engineering, and in 1876 he patented his invention of the "telephone". It had an important impact upon communication.

prompt:
Q: Why is gold the most electrically conductive metal?
A:
output:
Gold atoms are extremely efficient at conducting electricity, and they can absorb electrons from another atom. This ability makes it an excellent conductor of electrical current.
As a result of this property, gold is used in many industries as well as in the aerospace industry for electronic applications where extreme conductivity is required.
In addition to its use in electronics, other uses include jewelry making and dentistry because of its malleability and ductility.
